code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # simple verb mapping for readability
    verb_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of",
        "location": "is located at",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayName": "has runway named",
        "country": "is in",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has elevation of",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code",
        "nativeName": "has native name",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to party",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has elevation of",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement",
        "mayor": "has mayor",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has second runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has third runway surface of",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface of",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language",
        "city": "is city of",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter at",
        "class": "belongs to class",
        "division": "belongs to division",
        "order": "belongs to order",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft",
        "battle": "participated in battle",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fifth runway surface of",
        "countySeat": "has county seat",
        "chief": "has chief",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "areaCode": "has area code",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county",
    }
    # Group predicates by subject and order them for a more natural sentence
    from collections import defaultdict

    # Define a preferred order for predicates to improve readability
    predicate_order = [
        "cityServed", "regionServed", "country", "isPartOf", "location", "administrativeArrondissement",
        "largestCity", "countySeat", "capital", "mayor", "leader", "leaderParty", "leaderTitle",
        "operatingOrganisation", "owner", "foundingYear", "foundedBy", "headquarter",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet",
        "runwayLength", "1stRunwayLengthMetre", "1stRunwayLengthFeet", "2ndRunwayLengthFeet",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet", "4thRunwayLengthFeet", "5thRunwayLengthFeet",
        "runwayName", "runwaySurfaceType", "1stRunwaySurfaceType", "2ndRunwaySurfaceType",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType", "4thRunwaySurfaceType", "5thRunwaySurfaceType",
        "1stRunwayNumber", "2ndRunwayNumber", "3rdRunwayNumber", "4thRunwayNumber", "5thRunwayNumber",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier", "iataLocationIdentifier", "locationIdentifier",
        "nativeName", "officialLanguage", "language", "currency", "demonym",
        "aircraftHelicopter", "transportAircraft", "aircraftFighter", "attackAircraft",
        "battle", "hubAirport", "regionServed", "city", "jurisdiction", "postalCode",
        "areaCode", "ceremonialCounty"
    ]

    # Helper to get order index (large number for unknown predicates)
    order_index = {p: i for i, p in enumerate(predicate_order)}

    subject_data = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        subject_data[t.subject].append((order_index.get(t.predicate, len(predicate_order)), verb, t.object))

    sentences = []
    for subj, items in subject_data.items():
        # Sort predicates by the predefined order
        items.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
        # Build phrase strings
        phrases = [f"{verb} {obj}" for _, verb, obj in items]
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            sentences.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}")
        else:
            # Join all but the last with commas, add 'and' before the final phrase
            joined = ", ".join(phrases[:-1])
            sentences.append(f"{subj} {joined}, and {phrases[-1]}")
    # Combine clauses for different subjects into a single complex sentence
    return "; ".join(sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
